regime_justification: USDJPY hovering near 158.5 after early-May intervention, BOJ board members signaling June hike likely, JGB 10Y spiking to 2.7%, oil at $106 on Hormuz closure, but immediate intervention pressure cooled — mixed carry stress with elevated policy/intervention risk.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY faded post-intervention rally and sits 1.5 yen below the 160 watch line; BOJ Summary of Opinions published May 12 explicitly flags June hike as possible; JGB 10Y at 2.7% (highest since 1997); MOF burned one of three allowed IMF intervention windows May 1–7, signaling high alert.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ June 16 meeting 4 weeks out; pair at 158.5 still well below 160 intervention line; vol subdued; no imminent catalyst but setup fragile.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Approaching June BOJ; if USDJPY drifts back toward 159.5+ on hot US data or oil spike, MOF rate-checks return and intervention risk climbs sharply.

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: June 16 BOJ decision (3.5 weeks out); member Masu called for hike 'as soon as possible'; OECD estimates 2% terminal by 2027; market pricing 60% chance of 1.0% hike; surprise hawkish tone could trigger sharp unwind.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Post-BOJ clarity should reduce tail risk if hike delivered as priced; but if BOJ holds and oil stays >$100, carry unwind probability resets higher into July.

bottom_line: Stage-2 pre-intervention holding pattern: USDJPY at 158.5, JGB 10Y spiked to 2.7%, oil $106, BOJ member Masu explicitly calling for June hike, MOF used first of three IMF-allowed intervention windows, and Summary of Opinions published this week signaled board momentum building for 1.0% rate. Spread still 187 bps but JGB breakout and hawkish BOJ rhetoric are compressing carry-to-vol. June 16 BOJ meeting is the main event risk inside 4 weeks. Grades: C/C/B/C — elevated but not imminent, with 3-week spike around BOJ decision risk.

trigger_map.note: Member Masu (May 14): hike 'desirable at earliest stage possible'; Summary of Opinions: 'quite possible' from next meeting; 3 dissenting votes at April meeting wanted 1.0%; market ~60% priced.
Intervened May 1 (~¥5.4trn) & May 7 (~¥4.7trn); used 1 of 3 allowed IMF windows through Nov; USDJPY at 158.5 vs 160 watch line — will reload if pair tests 159.5+.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: 2.70% is highest since 1997; broke April Summary threshold that 'could trigger' tightening; BOJ QT ongoing, no YCC floor, private demand weak — structural breakout underway.
¥16,740/bbl now (WTI $106 × 158.5); Hormuz closed, Trump-Iran talks stalled; IEA warns supply gap through Oct; BOJ April Summary explicitly cited oil as upside inflation risk.
79.87 today; elevated post-CPI/PPI but below stress threshold; watching treasury selloff acceleration.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY faded 0.3% to 158.49, JGB 10Y spiked 5 bps to 2.70% (highest since 1997), US 10Y jumped 14 bps to 4.60% on hot CPI/PPI, WTI climbed 4.4% to $106 as Hormuz remains shut. BOJ board member Masu spoke Thursday (May 14) in Kagoshima, calling for rate hike 'at earliest stage possible if no clear economic downturn,' echoing hawkish tone in the Summary of Opinions released May 12 that flagged June hike as 'quite possible.' Nikkei sold off 2% Friday on chip weakness and rising BOJ hike fears. MOF has used 1 of 3 allowed IMF intervention windows (May 1 & 7); USDJPY at 158.5 is 1.5 yen below the 160 watch line but authorities remain on alert. Trump-Xi Beijing summit produced no Hormuz breakthrough; oil remains structurally bid. Stage holding at 2 (Pre-Intervention) — intervention pressure cooled but BOJ policy risk escalating into June 16 decision.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 190 bps (widened 9 bps despite JGB spike); carry-to-vol proxy ~24.7 (190 bps ÷ 7.7% vol) still positive but compressing as JGB breaks out structurally. USDJPY 5-day pace -0.4% (orderly post-intervention fade), 60-day mean ~158.8 so spot is neutral. ¥/bbl crude ¥16,740 is cycle high, embedding sustained import-cost pressure. JGB 10Y at 2.70% is 27-year high and crossed the April Summary threshold that policymakers said 'could warrant tightening.' BOJ member Masu (May 14) and Summary of Opinions (May 12) both explicitly signal June 16 hike as likely; OECD projects 2% terminal by end-2027; market pricing ~60% chance of 1.0% hike. April meeting saw 3 dissenting votes for immediate hike (Takata, Tamura, Nakagawa) — largest split under Ueda. We remain Stage 2: MOF burned one intervention window, USDJPY below the line but setup fragile, JGB breaking out, and June BOJ meeting 31 days out is the dominant catalyst. Grades 1-2w hold at C (watchful): no imminent trigger but any drift toward 159.5 brings MOF rate-checks back; vol is too low given realized stress. Grade 3w lifts to B (likely): June 16 decision risk is high, board momentum building, and hawkish surprise could trigger sharp unwind. Grade 4w steps back to C: post-BOJ clarity should decompress tail risk if hike delivered, though if BOJ disappoints and oil stays >$100, unwind probability resets higher into July. Applied unwind prior to 3w grade; pure data would support C but catalyzed event risk inside window justifies B.
narrative.p3: Bitcoin at $78,060 (-3.0% today, -3.1% 7d) failed three attempts to break $82k and is underperforming equities (SPX near ATH). Open interest and flows suggest rotation out of crypto toward real assets (gold, energy) and defensive positioning. BTC-DXY 30d correlation likely positive (DXY +0.1% as BTC sold off), typical of risk-off repricing. Crypto liquidity is draining: no ETF bid momentum, institutional caution rising as macro vol picks up, and levered long positions clearing out. The $78k–$80k range is now resistance; if USDJPY unwinds sharply or equity vol spikes, BTC likely tests $72k–$75k as carry unwind hits crypto leverage. Risk-on narrative is broken — equities grinding on AI hype but crypto, EM FX, and HY spreads all flashing caution. For positioning: any pop toward $82k is a fade until macro clarity (BOJ decision, Hormuz resolution, or Fed dovish turn).

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-week grade C holds at 2-week: no scheduled catalysts, USDJPY below intervention line, vol subdued. Main risk is exogenous oil spike or US data surprise pushing pair back toward 159.5, which would bring MOF rate-checks and verbal intervention. BOJ June meeting is 4 weeks out so no immediate policy catalyst. Spread 190 bps still supports carry but JGB breakout is structural headwind.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2-week C lifts to 3-week B: June 16 BOJ decision (31 days, inside 3w window) is the dominant risk event. Summary of Opinions (May 12) and Masu speech (May 14) both flag hike as likely; 3 board members already dissented for 1.0% in April; market pricing 60% chance. If BOJ hikes to 1.0% and signals more to come, carry math compresses fast and levered positions unwind. Even a hold with hawkish tone (e.g., 'July likely') could trigger positioning flush. Applied unwind prior here: data alone supports high-C, but catalyzed event risk justifies B.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3-week B steps back to 4-week C: assuming BOJ delivers hike as expected (or holds dovish), the immediate tail risk decompresses. Market will reprice terminal rate and vol should ease post-event. However, if BOJ disappoints (holds with no clear forward guidance) and oil remains >$100, unwind risk resets higher into July meeting (July 30–31). The 4-week window spans the BOJ decision plus 1 week of post-decision price action, so grades reflect 'most likely path' = hike delivered, clarity provided, vol eases.

watch_list: BOJ June 16 decision and Ueda press conference tone (hike to 1.0% priced ~60%; hawkish surprise = unwind trigger)
USDJPY drift back toward 159.5 (MOF will reload verbal intervention and rate-checks; only 2 IMF windows left through Nov)
WTI/Brent momentum above $110 or Hormuz breakthrough (¥/bbl already at cycle high; further spike forces BOJ hand; resolution eases import inflation)
US May CPI/jobs data (hot prints widen spread further, keep Fed hike risk alive, pressure USDJPY higher into BOJ meeting)
JGB 10Y acceleration through 2.8% (would signal private sector capitulation on BOJ put; levered JGB longs unwinding)
